mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding the 2010-2013 average trend compresses equity risk premiums by confirming durable demand expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high-GDP-growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate reading dropping below 7.0 percent signals rapid labor market normalization, which boosts consumer confidence and corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 7.0% in a single monthly release
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE remains above 6.5% for consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in housing starts and industrial production indicate synchronized demand and supply expansion, which reduces margin compression fears and lifts equity valuations over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative or zero directional accuracy over a 3-year out-of-sample test, or INDPRO declines while HOUST rises without generating positive forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: In a sustained monetary tightening cycle, rising federal funds rates combined with sticky core inflation compress equity duration multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in over 60% of tightening episodes, or CPIAUCSL decouples from FEDFUNDS movements without breaking the negative return correlation.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly jump in industrial production exceeding 0.5 percent signals strong manufacturing momentum and capacity utilization, which immediately lifts cyclical equity valuations and drives positive 5-day forward returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of instances where INDPRO exceeds the 0.5% threshold, or the signal fails to outperform a random baseline over a 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reinforces earnings stability expectations, which systematically reduces equity risk premiums and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day holding period.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in months with payroll growth above 150k are not statistically distinct from flat-growth months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales revenue coinciding with elevated federal funds rates indicates demand-driven inflation pressures that force prolonged monetary restraint, which suppresses equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces positive 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of co-occurring instances, or RSAFS declines while FEDFUNDS rises without triggering negative equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, rising housing starts paired with accelerating real GDP growth signal a durable demand rebound, which rapidly re-rates cyclical equities and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of post-shock recovery episodes identified by GDP and housing data, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold baseline over a 2-year walk-forward test.
